headline: Whipping punishment carried out on 1,460 prisoners in 2024
context: Malaysian prisons administered judicial whipping on 1,460 prisoners in 2024, with human rights groups calling for abolition of corporal punishment.

cards.big: 1,460 prisoners subjected to judicial whipping in Malaysian prisons in 2024
cards.sub: Corporal punishment remains legal for over 40 offences under federal law

cards.big: Criminal Procedure Code Sections 286-291 govern judicial whipping procedures
cards.sub: Maximum of 24 strokes for adults, 10 for juveniles under current law

cards.big: UN Committee Against Torture classified judicial caning as cruel punishment in 2019
cards.sub: Malaysia maintains reservation to CAT Article 1 on definition of torture

cards.big: 67% of whipping sentences in 2024 related to immigration and drug offences
cards.sub: Migrant workers disproportionately subjected to corporal punishment

cards.big: 1,460 state-inflicted whippings annually is institutionalised violence, not justice
Malaysian Medical Association called for health impact review of whipping in 2024
